slides.eyebrow: Proof · The vetting milestone — detailed feedback
slides.headline: 20 applications. 6 vetted.
Here's the detailed feedback we gave the 14 who didn't make it — because rejection without explanation is useless.
The 14 we didn't accept were rejected for readiness, not music.
Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: 9/14 had rights issues · Labels holding masters with no clear route to clearance · Uncleared samples embedded in commercial-quality work · Publishing registered with the wrong entity
list_items: Labels holding masters with no clear route to clearance
Uncleared samples embedded in commercial-quality work
Publishing registered with the wrong entity

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: 8/14 had metadata problems · No ISRC codes assigned · No BPM, key, or mood data · Files with no embedded information
list_items: No BPM, key, or mood data

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: 6/14 had file issues · Only MP3 available · No stems exported or accessible · Files not named to any standard
list_items: Files not named to any standard

slides.headline:
2/14 had response issues
→ 3+ day reply time in the response-time test scenario
slides.lede: Most of these overlap. Most are fixable.

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: What each rejection reason actually means · How to fix rights issues (and what takes longest) · The metadata checklist you need on every file · File format requirements for sync

slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
